pattern: Two structurally identical trees built from the same shape can be walked in lockstep, so the step that locates a node in one tree hands you its mirror position in the other for free.
hints: Walking the original tree alone only tells you where the target is in the original — you need to walk both trees at once.
Recurse (or queue) through the original and cloned trees together, moving into left/right of both at the same time.
The instant the original pointer equals the target, the cloned pointer you are holding at that same step is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: target is an interior node reached via the right subtree
single-node tree, target is the root
left-skewed chain — a solution that forgets to recurse right will still pass here, isolating left-only bugs
target lives only in the right subtree — catches a solution that short-circuits on the left search
value 0 at the target node guards against falsy-value bugs
negative values throughout, target deep on the left
target is the deepest leaf of an unbalanced tree

stress.description: random tree shape with a random target node
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    vals = rng.sample(range(-1000, 1001), n)
    def build(items):
        if not items:
            return None
        if len(items) == 1:
            return [items[0], None, None]
        i = rng.randrange(len(items))
        root_val = items[i]
        rest = items[:i] + items[i + 1:]
        rng.shuffle(rest)
        split = rng.randint(0, len(rest))
        return [root_val, build(rest[:split]), build(rest[split:])]
    tree = build(vals)
    out = []
    queue = [tree]
    while queue:
        node = queue.pop(0)
        if node is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(node[0])
        queue.append(node[1])
        queue.append(node[2])
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    target = rng.choice(vals)
    return [out, None, target]
